rationale_for_baseline_tier: An afebrile, haemodynamically stable patient with mild chronic benign neutropenia and an anxiety-driven adrenergic episode does not need emergency or same-day care. Routine GP review within a week is appropriate — BUT paired with a mandatory neutropenic-fever safety-net, because a future measured fever would escalate immediately to T4/T5.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Measured fever >=38.0C, true shaking rigors, or new signs of infection/bleeding
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend ED promptly and state that white-cell counts run low
escalation_edges.rationale: A fever in a neutropenic patient is potential neutropenic sepsis needing urgent antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your temperature reaches 38C or higher, or you get real shaking chills, go to the emergency department and tell them your white cells are low — this needs quick treatment.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for ED assessment if a true fever develops; give written neutropenic-fever advice.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is not an emergency tonight — you have no measured fever and no signs of infection. Get a thermometer, use slow breathing to settle, and see your GP within a week. If your temperature hits 38C or you get shaking chills, go to ED and mention your low white cells.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; ED if a measured fever develops
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight if a measured fever >=38.0C, rigors, bleeding, or new infection symptoms develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of anxiety response to an incidental benign-neutropenia result; afebrile, no infective focus; neutropenic-fever safety-net given; routine FBC/haematology follow-up advised.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without any neutropenic-fever safety-net or temperature advice.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A true fever later develops overnight, is not recognised as an emergency, and neutropenic sepsis progresses untreated.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending an afebrile anxious patient to ED/ambulance as presumed sepsis is over-triage and reinforces catastrophic beliefs.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: No measured temperature available and cannot examine for an infective focus remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Advise obtaining a thermometer and give a firm neutropenic-fever safety-net; reassure only about the afebrile present state.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Assume the subjective chills are (or are not) a fever without a measurement.
